title: allium enginii
intro: Allium enginii, also known as the Turkish garlic or wild garlic, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Allium genus, which includes various types of garlic and onions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Allium enginii include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties. It is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and supplements, which can be prepared from its leaves, flowers, and roots.
